criteria.text: normalize_pdf returns page_count as part of its result (or a lightweight accompanying value)
criteria.planned_check: normalize_pdf returns NormalizedPdfText(str) instance with .page_count attribute set from len(blocks), computed with zero extra pass; verified by new test asserting text.page_count == expected page count immediately after calling normalize_pdf directly (inside the new dispatch test) and by code inspection (no extra fitz/regex pass introduced).

criteria.text: dispatch.py's dispatch_pdf uses page_count directly instead of sum(1 for _ in iter_pages(text))'s redundant second O(n) pass
criteria.planned_check: dispatch_pdf's body no longer calls iter_pages; verified by new test monkeypatching ingestion.normalize_pdf.iter_pages to raise AssertionError if invoked, then calling dispatch_pdf and asserting it completes without error.

criteria.text: Test spec: pytest agents/ingestion/test_dispatch.py -k page_count: assert dispatch_pdf's reported page_count matches normalize_pdf's own count with no second parse
criteria.planned_check: Run exactly this pytest command post-implementation; both the pre-existing test_dispatch_pdf_structured_fields_page_count and the new test_dispatch_pdf_page_count_matches_normalize_pdf_no_second_parse must pass.

criteria.text: No existing behavior/tests broken by changing normalize_pdf's return value
criteria.planned_check: Run full pytest agents/ingestion/ -v suite; all pre-existing tests (including test_normalize_pdf.py's ~14 tests that treat normalize_pdf's result as a plain str) must still pass unmodified.

criteria.text: Removing the sum(1 for _ in iter_pages(...)) call must not leave an unused import
criteria.planned_check: ruff check agents/ingestion/ after removing iter_pages from dispatch.py's import line.
